architecture_summary: ViTPose employs plain and non-hierarchical vision transformers as backbones to extract features for a given person instance and a lightweight decoder for pose estimation. It uses masked image modeling pre-training, lightweight deconvolution/bilinear decoders, and supports scalable backbones, flexible attention types, multi-dataset training, and knowledge-token transfer.

novel_contributions: a simple yet effective baseline model named ViTPose for human pose estimation
ViTPose can be scaled up from 100M to 1B parameters
ViTPose is very flexible regarding the attention type, input resolution, pre-training and finetuning strategy, as well as dealing with multiple pose tasks
the knowledge of large ViTPose models can be easily transferred to small ones via a simple knowledge token
a single ViTPose model with a big backbone ViTAE-G obtains the best 80.9 AP on the MS COCO test-dev set

key_claims.claim: ViTPose is a simple baseline using plain vision transformers and a lightweight decoder for human pose estimation, without elaborate structural designs.
ViTPose can be scaled up from 100M to 1B parameters and sets a new Pareto front between throughput and performance.
The model is flexible with respect to attention type, input resolution, pre-training and finetuning strategy, and multiple pose tasks.
Large ViTPose models can transfer knowledge to smaller ones via a simple knowledge token.
ViTPose achieves state-of-the-art 80.9 AP on the MS COCO test-dev set, and the paper explicitly notes the largest model sets a new state-of-the-art.
Using the simple decoder causes only marginal performance drops for ViT-B, ViT-L, and ViT-H, unlike the large drops seen for ResNet backbones.
With multi-dataset training and a ViTAE-G backbone, ViTPose reaches 80.9 AP on MS COCO test-dev and 81.1 AP with ensembling three models.
